title: rubus idaeus var. maritimus
intro: Rubus idaeus var. maritimus, also known as coastal raspberry or sea raspberry, is a variety of the raspberry plant that grows in coastal areas and has adapted to thrive in harsh marine environments. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been found to have antimicrobial, antiviral, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Rubus idaeus var. maritimus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against certain types of cancer, due to its high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for medicinal purposes, although it can also be consumed fresh or dried for its nutritional value.
